commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow code/test repair in tart Screen Sharing enablement, suitable for an automated fix attempt if maintainers want one.; Needs attention: No secrets are passed to the guest, but the host-local Screen Sharing exposure should be an explicit maintainer-approved boundary.; Review finding: [P2] Fail when Screen Sharing does not actually start — internal/providers/tart/backend.go:541-543 (sha=55a116900da77c4beeeb7373ed3bba8a7734adab)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds tart `--desktop` support by advertising `FeatureDesktop`, enabling macOS Screen Sharing during acquire, guarding WebVNC for macOS leases, adding tests, and updating tart docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect from source inspection: all Screen Sharing start commands are followed by `|| true`, so the script exits successfully even when the service is not enabled. The happy path has live proof, but the failure path is not guarded.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow code/test repair in tart Screen Sharing enablement, suitable for an automated fix attempt if maintainers want one.

target.title: feat(tart): desktop/VNC support via guest Screen Sharing
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to the tart provider (#209), which shipped headless. `crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop` now brings up a visible macOS session by enabling the guest's built-in **Screen Sharing** (native VNC on port 5900); connect with a native VNC client over an SSH tunnel.

## How it works

- Advertise `FeatureDesktop` so `--desktop` is accepted.
- During Acquire, for `--desktop` leases, enable `com.apple.screensharing` in the guest via `tart exec`. The VM still runs `--no-graphics`. Auth uses the **guest account's own credentials** (e.g. cirruslabs `admin`); crabbox writes no VNC password and passes no secret to the guest.

```sh
crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop
# local (on the Mac):
ssh -i <lease-key> -L 5900:127.0.0.1:5900 admin@<lease-ip>; open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900
# remote control plane (controller -> Mac -> guest):
ssh -L 5900:<guest-ip>:5900 <user>@<mac>; open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900
```

## Review-feedback resolutions

- **WebVNC routing guarded.** Advertising `FeatureDesktop` would route `crabbox webvnc --provider tart` into `directSSHWebVNC`, whose noVNC/`websockify` bridge is Linux-only. It now **rejects macOS leases with native-client guidance** (`guardMacOSDirectWebVNC`, focused routing test). Live: `crabbox webvnc --provider tart` prints the `ssh -L 5900` / `vnc://` steps and exits non-zero.
- **Exposure boundary stated.** macOS Screen Sharing binds all guest interfaces, so VNC is reachable at the guest's address on the **host-local tart network**, gated by account auth; the SSH tunnels keep the viewer on `127.0.0.1`. Documented explicitly.
- **No credential exposure / accurate docs.** No managed VNC password is written or passed via argv; docs corrected.

## Live proof — Apple Silicon M4 Pro, tart 2.32.1, macOS 26.4

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop --slug vncn
provisioned lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-vncn-… state=ready ; ssh=admin@192.168.64.11:22
# RFB handshake to guest 5900:
server banner: RFB 003.889
offered security types: [(30,Apple/Mac-auth),(33),(36),(35)]   # real authenticating server

$ crabbox webvnc --provider tart --id cbx_…
webvnc's browser bridge requires Linux noVNC tooling and is not available for macOS leases;
connect a native VNC client over an SSH tunnel instead:
  ssh -L 5900:127.0.0.1:5900 admin@<lease-ip>
  open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900                                    # guard rejects cleanly

$ crabbox list --provider tart    -> crabbox-vncn-… ready target=macos
$ crabbox stop --provider tart …  -> released ; verify: 0 in crabbox list AND tart list
```

## Files

`internal/providers/tart/{provider,backend,flags}.go`, `internal/cli/webvnc.go` (guard), tests, `docs/providers/tart.md`.

## Not included (follow-up)

- A macOS noVNC bridge so `crabbox webvnc` works browser-side for tart (esp. the remote controller→Mac→guest case).
- Resolution/scaling controls.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
